finding_results.notes: Replaced the clean 'my mother + chemotherapy in Delhi + visa + can't send anything back' ladder (former line 195) with a fragmented approach: Priya starts with her visa, breaks off mid-sentence, restarts through the relay/mother channel trailing into an unfinished clause ('I cannot even —'). Replaced Noor's polished thesis line ('I needed one person in this building whose judgment wasn't compromised') with a messier exchange: Priya says she came to Noor because she thought Noor 'still saw clearly' (self-interrupting twice before landing the phrase), Noor deflects with 'I was trying to,' and Priya delivers the verdict 'That is not what I needed you to be' — which arrives as personal need rather than abstract moral summary. Three character-true interruptions/self-corrections now route Priya to the family and trust stakes. No mention of chemotherapy. The 'cannot send anything back' formulation is gone. Priya's low-contraction register preserved (uncontracted 'cannot,' 'is not'). Existing face-closing beat and closing exchange ('I don't know what to tell you' / 'You don't need to tell me anything') retained unchanged.

summary: Single finding addressed. Priya's confrontation scene revoiced to eliminate the calibration-echo progression and Noor's thesis-clean judgment line. The revised exchange routes Priya to her family and visa stakes through three character-true self-corrections and interrupted clauses, preserving her formal-leaning register and the scene's emotional facts while breaking the polished set-piece shape. No other chapter material was altered.
